author.signature:
The 2-week ux design sprint for designers who are tired of shitty bootcamps. 
and freebies that will save you 100+ design hours 
🌎yummy-labs.com

contents.desc: You’re leaving so much on the table. You need a single markdown file that teaches Claude how you think.
Not prompts you paste every time. Not "act as a senior designer." An actual instruction set that Claude loads automatically whenever design work comes up.
I built two:
→ A UX Designer skill (heuristics, psychology, "why before what" thinking, trade-off reasoning) → A UI Designer skill (visual craft, spacing systems, component design, responsive patterns)

The difference is wild. Before: Claude was a pixel generator that did what I said. After: it asks clarifying questions, flags heuristic violations, designs within my token system, and pushes back when something doesn't make sense.
This is Claude Design Skills #1: I'll be breaking down more ways to make Claude a better design partner in this series.
Want both SKILL.md files? They're free.
Comment "SKILLS" and I'll DM them to you.

author.signature: Learn Figma, UI & AI to streamline your workflow & grow as a designer. 02UI.com

contents.desc: Can Claude Learn Your Design Workflow?
There is a GitHub library called Awesome Claude Skills with over 32000 stars. Developers are building workflows for tools like Figma, Webflow, Canva and design systems.
You do not need to write code to use it.
You need to explain how you work.
In this video I break down why designers who think clearly about their own workflow will benefit the most.
You will learn how to describe your components, style guidelines and process so Claude can adapt to your way of working.
If you can explain your design system in simple steps, you can turn AI into a real assistant.
Try this. Open one of your Figma files. Write down how you build a button component from scratch. That is where this starts.

itemList.desc: Can Claude Learn Your Design Workflow? There is a GitHub library called Awesome Claude Skills with over 32000 stars. In this video I break down why designers who think clearly about their own workflow will benefit the most. You will learn how to describe your components, style guidelines and process so Claude can adapt to your way of working. If you can explain your design system in simple steps, you can turn AI into a real assistant. Try this. Open one of your Figma files. Write down how you build a button component from scratch. That is where this starts.

contents.desc: Everyone talks about AI agents.
Almost nobody is talking about architecture.
If your agents don’t have roles, memory layers, and rules…
you don’t have a system > you have chaos.
Who executes?
Who routes?
Who observes?
Who remembers?
That’s where things get interesting.
This is what a real multi-agent setup starts to look like when you stop thinking “chatbot” and start thinking “workforce”.
#aisystems #openclaw #aiworkflow #agenticengineering #aiautomation
